rationale: Both papers are in the same FMCG promotion-competition domain, and the cited study provides a well-known empirical generalization on market-share-based cross-price asymmetry that is plausibly relevant to the citing paper’s focus on cross-brand heterogeneity (including small vs. large brands) in promotional effects. Given this topical and construct overlap (price promotions, brand share differences, competitive response), this does not look like an obvious incidental mention from the available evidence.
